questions.original_question: Who does the Zeekr board of directors consist of?
questions.answer:
As of the latest available information, the board of directors of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited includes the following members: **Shufu Li** as Chairman of the Board, **Conghui An** as Director and Chief Executive Officer, **Donghui Li** as Director, **Shengyue Gui** as Director, and **Jing Yuan** as Chief Financial Officer. These individuals hold key leadership roles within the company, contributing to its strategic direction and operational success.

The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

The compensation committee assists the board of directors in reviewing and approving the compensation structure, including all forms of compensation, relating to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's directors and executive officers. The chief executive officer may not be present at any committee meeting during which their compensation is deliberated upon. The compensation committee is responsible for, among other things: reviewing and approving the compensation of each of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's executive officers; periodically reviewing ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's management succession planning, including policies for executive officers’ selection and succession in the event of the incapacitation, retirement or removal of the executive officers, and evaluations of, and development plans for, any potential successors to the executive officers; reviewing and submitting for the board’s approval ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's executive compensation and benefits policies generally; periodically reviewing and assessing the adequacy of the committee charter and recommending any proposed changes to the board for approval; and   
reviewing and assessing risks arising from ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's employee compensation policies and practices and whether any such risks are reasonably likely to have a material adverse effect on ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited. Nominating Committee. The nominating committee consists of Shengyue Gui, Donghui Li, Stephen Brown Davis, and Michael David Ricks, and is chaired by Shengyue Gui. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited has determined that Stephen Brown Davis and Michael David Ricks satisfy the “independence” requirements of Rule 5605(a)(2) of the Listing Rules of the NYSE.

rag_info.chunk_content: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (the “Company” or “Zeekr”) was incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021. The Company, together with its subsidiaries (collectively, the “Group”), is principally engaged in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, as well as the provision of automotive-related research and development services. Prior to the establishment of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, the Group’s businesses were conducted through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., previously known as Shanghai Maple Guorun Automobile Company Ltd. (“Zeekr Shanghai”); (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“Zeekr Hangzhou Bay”); (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”); (iv) ZEEKR Technology Europe AB (“ZTE”, previously known as China-Euro Vehicle Technology Aktiebolag or “CEVT”). In 2021, in an effort to establish an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to obtain investment from outside investors and in preparation for an overseas listing, Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd. (“Geely Holding”), the controlling shareholder of Geely Automobile Holdings Limited (“Geely Auto”), undertook a series of contemplated reorganizations (the “Reorganization”). The main purpose of the Reorganization was to establish ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited as the overseas holding company for the above BEV-related businesses that were originally operated within Geely Holding and Geely Auto. Subsequent to the establishment of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited in March 2021, the above businesses were transferred to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited by Geely Holding and Geely Auto as part of the Reorganization.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology began as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017. Lotus Technology conducts its business primarily through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Shanghai”), (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay”), (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”) and (iv) Zeekr Technology Europe AB (“CEVT”). Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology was incorporated as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as the holding company. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology is seeking to list separately from Geely Auto because of its different brand positioning and its operational, management, and financial independence. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology has an equity story built around its premium brand, product portfolio, and future plans that is better served by operating independently and seeking a separate listing, which the company believes will allow it to establish its own profile and attract different investors. In April 2021, ZEEKR Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, ZEEKR Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, ZEEKR 001, and started delivery from October 2021.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Zeekr’s smart cockpit is powered by Zeekr OS, an exclusive distributed operating system designed for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), seamlessly integrated with Zeekr's state-of-the-art electrical and electronic (E/E) architecture. By dynamically allocating processing power to services, functions, and applications in real-time, Zeekr OS optimizes cockpit interactions, autonomous driving, and vehicle control, ensuring a seamless and highly responsive user experience. Zeekr OS features a unified service-oriented architecture, in-house full-stack research and development (R&D), optimized network diagnostics, and automated software integration testing, eliminating system lag and enabling continuous updates. With multi-kernel compatibility (Linux, QNX), Zeekr OS supports 360-degree surround-view monitoring, parking assistance, and intelligent energy management, setting a new benchmark for BEV software architecture. The smart cockpit experience is further enhanced by the Qualcomm 8295 SoC-based platform, standard across all Zeekr vehicle models launched in 2024, with the Zeekr 009 Grand featuring the world’s first dual-8295 configuration. The Zeekr Sound audio system, equipped with AI-powered tuning, delivers premium acoustic performance, while the Human-Machine Interface (HMI) 3.5 introduces 3D navigation, advanced visual effects, and an intuitive Eva family interface. EVA, the AI-powered driving assistant, leverages machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and computer vision to provide intelligent voice interaction, predictive assistance, and customizable AI personas. Additionally, Zeekr’s open cockpit ecosystem integrates a virtualized mobile platform, a Scenario Library, and Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity, allowing seamless integration with smart home devices, gaming, and entertainment applications such as Kid Zone, Tencent Cloud Gaming, and wireless karaoke.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Smart Cockpit 
summary: Zeekr's smart cockpit, set to launch in 2024, is powered by Zeekr OS, a specialized distributed operating system designed for battery electric vehicles (BEVs). This system integrates advanced electronic and electrical architecture, enabling real-time processing power allocation to enhance user interactions, autonomous driving, and vehicle control. Key features include a unified service-oriented architecture, in-house R&D, improved network diagnostics, and automated software integration testing, which together minimize system lag and support continuous updates. The multi-kernel compatibility allows for advanced functionalities like 360-degree surround-view monitoring and intelligent energy management, setting a new benchmark for BEV software.

The smart cockpit experience is further elevated by the Qualcomm 8295 SoC platform, particularly in the Zeekr 009 Grand, which boasts a dual-8295 configuration. The Zeekr Sound audio system delivers premium sound through AI-driven tuning, while the HMI 3.5 offers advanced 3D navigation and visual effects, enhanced by the intuitive Eva family interface. EVA, the AI assistant, leverages machine learning and natural language processing for intelligent voice interactions and customizable AI personas. The open cockpit ecosystem promotes a virtualized mobile platform and IoT connectivity, allowing integration with smart home devices and entertainment options like Kid Zone, Tencent Cloud Gaming, and wireless karaoke. Overall, Zeekr's smart cockpit, driven by Zeekr OS and AI advancements, aims to provide an immersive and connected driving experience.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Zeekr 001, Zeekr's first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV), is a premium crossover shooting brake developed with proprietary research and development (R&D), deep market insights, and the Sustainable Experience Architecture (SEA) platform. Launched in April 2021 with deliveries beginning in October 2021, the Zeekr 001 was the first mass-produced BEV with over $1,000 kilometers CLTC range. In October 2023, Zeekr introduced the Zeekr 001 FR, a high-performance variant featuring a lightweight carbon fiber body, 800V Qilin battery, and quad e-drive system, achieving 2.02 seconds 0-100 kilometers per hour acceleration and a 280 kilometers per hour top speed. In 2024, Zeekr launched upgraded versions of the Zeekr 001 with Zeekr Intelligent Driving 2.0 and significant performance, safety, and intelligence upgrades.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Zeekr 001 
summary: The Zeekr 001, launched in April 2021 as the first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV) on the SEA platform, is a premium crossover shooting brake known for its remarkable range of over 1,000 km on the CLTC cycle. In October 2023, the high-performance Zeekr 001 FR variant was unveiled, featuring a lightweight carbon fiber body, an 800V Qilin battery, and a quad e-drive system that enables acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.02 seconds, with a top speed of 280 km/h. In 2024, the Zeekr 001 received upgrades, including the Zeekr Intelligent Driving 2.0 system, enhancing its performance, safety, and intelligence.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, as detailed in the combined and consolidated statements for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, reveals significant changes in shareholders' equity and overall financial performance. The company began 2021 with no issued shares and total equity of RMB 3,379,969. Throughout the years, it underwent substantial transactions, including a capital injection of RMB 500,000 and the issuance of 2 billion ordinary shares, which contributed to a total paid-in capital of RMB 4,269,555 by the end of 2021. However, the company faced challenges, including a cumulative comprehensive loss of RMB 4,584,927, resulting in a total equity of RMB 929,426.

By 2023, Zeekr reported 2 billion ordinary shares and approximately 265.8 million preferred shares, with additional paid-in capital rising to RMB 11.2 billion. Despite these increases, the accumulated deficit escalated to RMB (20.9 billion), reflecting a net loss of RMB (8.3 billion) for the year. The total shareholders' equity decreased to RMB (8.7 billion), indicating ongoing financial difficulties.

The cash flow statements highlighted the company's struggles, with net losses from operating activities growing from RMB 4.5 billion in 2021 to RMB 8.3 billion in 2023. Share-based compensation and depreciation expenses also rose, underscoring the financial strain. The adjustments in accounts receivable and inventory further illustrated the challenges faced by Zeekr in managing its financial health.

Overall, the combined financial statements provide a comprehensive view of Zeekr's equity structure, financial performance, and cash flow dynamics, emphasizing the impact of various transactions and losses on the company's fiscal standing.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Zeekr Tech EU 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB (Zeekr Tech EU), established in 2013 in Sweden, serves as a research and development hub focused on creating innovative products for international markets. The organization specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and battery electric vehicles (BEVs) tailored to local standards, alongside digital offerings like data analytics and smart cockpit functions. A significant focus is placed on advancing BEV technologies that prioritize user-centric systems, integrating in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities with centralized architecture for improved user experiences.

Central to Zeekr Tech EU's initiatives is the SEA-M platform, an evolution of the SEA architecture designed to support autonomous driving and smart mobility. This platform aims to deliver versatile mobility options, exceptional driving experiences, and long-lasting vehicles that can be continuously upgraded. BEVs developed on this platform, such as the Zeekr Mix and Waymo RT robotaxi, feature innovative designs, including wide door openings and flexible interiors. By the end of 2024, the company employed 733 individuals, focusing on mobility technologies, digital solutions, and future product development.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the Group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, specializes in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, along with providing automotive-related research and development services through its subsidiaries. The company emerged from a reorganization initiated by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., which aimed to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to attract external investment and prepare for an overseas listing. This reorganization involved transferring various entities, including ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., and ZEEKR Technology Europe AB, under the common control of Geely Holding to Zeekr.

In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and subsequently acquired full equity stakes in Zeekr Hangzhou Bay and Zeekr Shanghai, incurring minor losses in additional paid-in capital. Additionally, Zeekr Shanghai acquired a 51% interest in Ningbo Viridi and later, in February 2022, ZTE from Geely Holding, further solidifying its operational structure. By May 2024, ZEEKR achieved a significant milestone by successfully listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), marking its growth and establishment in the electric vehicle market.

title: CEVT 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB, also known as CEVT, is a Swedish research and development center established in 2013, focusing on automotive innovation for international markets, particularly in Europe and the United States. CEVT specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and passenger battery electric vehicles (BEVs) that comply with local standards. The center is also developing advanced digital tools such as data analytics, smart cockpits, and fleet operations, while emphasizing user-centric systems that integrate in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities. 

One of CEVT's key projects is the SEA-M platform, an enhanced version of its SEA platform designed to support ZEEKR's future BEVs. This platform will feature autonomous driving capabilities and smart mobility options, promising a superior driving experience with innovative designs like a B-pillar-less structure and advanced air conditioning technologies. In July 2021, Zhejiang ZEEKR was incorporated in the People's Republic of China and is currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zeekr Innovation. In August 2021, ZEEKR Innovation acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Shanghai (99% from Geely Auto and 1% from Geely Holding). In October 2021, Lotus Technology acquired a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi, which was previously wholly-owned by Geely Holding. In February 2022, Lotus Technology acquired 100% equity interest in Zeekr Tech EU from Geely Holding.

summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology began as a division of Geely Auto in October 2017 and became a holding company in March 2021 under Cayman Islands law. metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the Group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, specializes in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, along with providing automotive-related research and development services through its subsidiaries. The company emerged from a reorganization initiated by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., which aimed to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to attract external investment and prepare for an overseas listing. This reorganization involved transferring various entities, including ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., and ZEEKR Technology Europe AB, under the common control of Geely Holding to Zeekr.

In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and subsequently acquired full equity stakes in Zeekr Hangzhou Bay and Zeekr Shanghai, incurring minor losses in additional paid-in capital. Additionally, Zeekr Shanghai acquired a 51% interest in Ningbo Viridi and later, in February 2022, ZTE from Geely Holding, further solidifying its operational structure. By May 2024, ZEEKR achieved a significant milestone by successfully listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), marking its growth and establishment in the electric vehicle market.

As a result of this transaction, Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. became a subsidiary of the Group with a 49% non-controlling interest in the amount of RMB743,088 recognized at the date of acquisition. The corresponding difference between acquisition consideration paid and the entity’s net book value of RMB13,130 loss was recognized in additional paid-in capital. In May 2024, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited successfully listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE).

title: Basis for Opinion 
summary: The financial statements of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited are prepared by the company's management, while an independent public accounting firm, Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Certified Public Accountants LLP, conducts audits to provide an opinion on these statements. Deloitte, a prominent member of the "Big Four" accounting firms, adheres to the standards set by the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (PCAOB) and U.S. federal securities laws to ensure independence and quality in its audit processes. The audits, conducted from 2021 to 2023, focused on assessing the risk of material misstatements in the financial statements, without expressing an opinion on the effectiveness of the company's internal control over financial reporting.

The consolidated balance sheet reveals significant growth in Zeekr's assets over the three years, with total assets increasing from RMB 11,939,932 in 2021 to RMB 27,117,500 in 2023 (approximately USD 3,819,420). Current assets rose notably, driven by increases in inventories and accounts receivable, while non-current assets also experienced substantial growth, particularly in property and equipment. The financial data is further supported by detailed notes that clarify accounting policies and significant transactions, enhancing the overall understanding of the company's financial position.

In addition to its financial aspects, Hangzhou, the capital of Zhejiang Province in China, serves as a backdrop for Zeekr's operations. Known for its rich cultural heritage and modern technological advancements, Hangzhou is home to major companies like Alibaba and offers a blend of historical and contemporary attractions, making it a vibrant hub for business and innovation.

In addition to its financial performance, Hangzhou, where Zeekr is based, is a city rich in history and culture, known for its scenic West Lake and as a center for technology and innovation, housing major companies like Alibaba. This blend of historical significance and modern development positions Hangzhou as a key player in the digital economy and tourism, enhancing the context of Zeekr's operations and growth potential. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology built an R&D team with extensive industry knowledge and experience. Over the years, Lotus Technology has recruited R&D talents from Geely Group as well as other established technology companies. The R&D team consists of members who are professional and recognize Lotus Technology's corporate goals. Lotus Technology has developed designated teams focused on vehicle-mounted technologies and R&D centers dedicated to autonomous driving, Zeekr OS, E/E Architecture, software, and internet. As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology's R&D team consisted of 7,492 professionals in fields such as vehicle development, vehicle design, electric vehicle structure development, and auto-pilot. As of the same date, Lotus Technology's R&D personnel specialized in software development and technology amounted to 5,629, representing 75.1% of the total R&D employees. Lotus Technology has established a global R&D network with multiple innovation centers specializing in different areas of expertise. Lotus Technology's R&D team in Europe unites top engineers globally, driving global innovation to meet diverse market demands. Additionally, Ningbo Viridi is dedicated to battery, electric drive, and charging system research, supporting Lotus Technology’s advancements in electrification. This integrated R&D ecosystem enables Lotus Technology to accelerate innovation and deliver cutting-edge battery electric vehicle solutions. In addition, Lotus Technology has set up two R&D centers in the People's Republic of China. The Lotus Technology R&D Center (Hangzhou Bay) focuses on vehicle development and chassis engineering, ensuring high-performance driving dynamics. Lotus Technology has been dedicated to serving its customers by leveraging top-notch technology, advanced product concepts, and an enriched entrepreneurial spirit that embraces creativity and innovation. ZEEKR is strategically positioned as a premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) brand that delivers an ultimate experience covering driving, charging, after-sale service, and customer community engagement. metadata.title_summary:
title: An Innovative Automotive Technology Company with Strong Product Offering to Capture Massive Premium BEV Market Opportunity 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company dedicated to creating premium electric vehicles and innovative solutions that promote the electrification and intelligentization of the automotive sector. Operating as an independent startup, ZEEKR utilizes its in-house research and development capabilities alongside a proprietary sales and marketing network, all managed by a diverse and efficient leadership team. Notably, while some executives have ties to Geely Auto, they will not hold executive roles post-offering, ensuring a distinct operational identity.

Since its launch less than three years ago, ZEEKR has introduced several models, including the ZEEKR 001, a five-seater crossover, the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, and the compact ZEEKR X SUV, among others. The brand has garnered significant market attention, achieving rapid sales milestones, such as 10,000 deliveries of the ZEEKR 001 shortly after its release, positioning it as one of China’s fastest-selling premium BEVs.

ZEEKR vehicles are built on Geely Holding's proprietary SEA platform, which facilitates swift development and launch of new models while ensuring exceptional performance and user experience. The ZEEKR 001 and ZEEKR 009 are recognized for their impressive range capabilities, with the ZEEKR 009 being the first pure-electric MPV to exceed 800 km in range. Additionally, ZEEKR incorporates advanced autonomous driving technologies powered by cutting-edge hardware, enhancing both performance and safety.

The company emphasizes customer satisfaction through a focus on innovative technology and premium features, such as customizable in-vehicle configurations and luxurious amenities in models like the ZEEKR 009 Grand, which includes heated massaging seats and high-quality entertainment systems. Overall, ZEEKR is committed to delivering a holistic smart mobility experience, setting a high standard in the premium BEV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Within less than three years since ZEEKR’s inception, ZEEKR has launched multiple commercialized electric vehicle models, namely ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001 is a five-seater crossover shooting brake BEV model targeting the premium market and mainly addressing the customer need for practical yet stylish traveling. ZEEKR released ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in February 2024 and started vehicle delivery in March 2024. ZEEKR 009 is a luxury six-seater MPV addressing the customer need for luxury mobility. In April 2023, ZEEKR released ZEEKR X, the compact SUV model, and began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. ZEEKR also started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023 and began to deliver the first upscale sedan model in January 2024. In addition to major upgrades such as the release of ZEEKR 001 (2024 model), ZEEKR has also been continuously fine-tuning and improving the performance of other BEV models. For example, ZEEKR updated the interior configurations of ZEEKR 009 and improved the chassis system of ZEEKR X in 2024. In April 2024, ZEEKR launched ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury version of ZEEKR 009 featuring enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence. ZEEKR also released ZEEKR MIX, the MPV model, in the same month.

(“Ningbo Viridi”) and (iv) Zeekr Technology Europe AB (“CEVT”). Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology was incorporated as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as the holding company. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology is seeking to list separately from Geely Auto because of its different brand positioning and its operational, management, and financial independence. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology has an equity story built around its premium brand, product portfolio, and future plans that is better served by operating independently and seeking a separate listing, which the company believes will allow it to establish its own profile and attract different investors. In April 2021, ZEEKR Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, ZEEKR Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, ZEEKR 001, and started delivery from October 2021.

title: Our Corporate History 
summary: ZEEKR originated as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017 and has since evolved into a distinct entity with a focus on premium electric vehicles. Established as ZEEKR Intelligent Technology in March 2021 in the Cayman Islands, the company was co-founded by Mr. Shufu Li and others to pursue operational and financial independence from Geely Auto, allowing it to cultivate its own brand identity and attract diverse investors. The organization operates through several subsidiaries, including ZEEKR Automobile and ZEEKR Technology, and has made significant acquisitions to enhance its capabilities, such as acquiring ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay and CEVT from Geely Holding.

ZEEKR launched its first battery electric vehicle, the ZEEKR 001, in April 2021, followed by the ZEEKR 009 in November 2022 and the ZEEKR X compact SUV in April 2023. The company continues to expand its product lineup, introducing models like the ZEEKR 001 FR in October 2023 and an upscale sedan in January 2024. In April 2024, ZEEKR unveiled the ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury variant, and the ZEEKR MIX, a multi-purpose vehicle. This strategic focus on premium offerings and innovative designs positions ZEEKR as a competitive player in the electric vehicle market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established in the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021, is dedicated to the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles (EVs) and batteries, alongside providing automotive-related research and development services. The company emerged from a restructuring of operations previously managed by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., with the aim of creating a focused battery electric vehicle (BEV) business to attract investment and facilitate overseas listings.

The ZEEKR Group comprises several entities, each with specific roles in the EV ecosystem. Key entities include ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., an investment holding company; ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., which handles EV production and commercialization; Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., focused on electric powertrains and battery packs; and Zeekr Technology Europe AB in Sweden, specializing in research and development.

The restructuring process involved transferring relevant businesses to ZEEKR, which was recognized as a holding company for BEV operations previously under Geely Holding. In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and acquired ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay from Geely Holding for RMB485.3 million, followed by the acquisition of ZEEKR Shanghai for RMB980.4 million in August. These transactions, treated as restructuring under common control, aimed to streamline operations and enhance ZEEKR's strategic focus on advancing electric vehicle commercialization and supporting related activities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's board of directors will consist of eight directors, including four independent directors, namely Stephen Brown Davis, Miguel A. Lopez Ben, Latha Maripuri, and Michael David Ricks, upon the SEC’s declaration of effectiveness of Lotus Technology's registration statement on Form F-1 to which this prospectus forms a part. A director is not required to hold any shares in Lotus Technology to qualify to serve as a director. The Listing Rules of the NYSE generally require that a majority of an issuer’s board of directors must consist of independent directors. However, the Listing Rules of the NYSE permit foreign private issuers like Lotus Technology to follow “home country practice” in certain corporate governance matters. Lotus Technology relies on this “home country practice” exception and does not have a majority of independent directors serving on its board of directors. A director who is in any way, whether directly or indirectly, interested in a contract or proposed contract with Lotus Technology is required to declare the nature of his or her interest at a meeting of Lotus Technology's board of directors.

summary: The board of directors will consist of eight members, including four independent directors: Stephen Brown Davis, Miguel A. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Upon the completion of this offering, Lotus Technology will be a “controlled company” as defined under the NYSE Listed Company Manual. For so long as Lotus Technology remains a controlled company, Lotus Technology may rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance rules, including (i) the requirement that a majority of the board of directors consist of independent directors, (ii) the requirement that the compensation of Lotus Technology's officers be determined or recommended to the board of directors by a compensation committee that is comprised solely of independent directors, and (iii) the requirement that director nominees be selected or recommended to the board of directors by a majority of independent directors or a nominating committee comprised solely of independent directors. Currently, Lotus Technology does not plan to utilize the exemptions available for controlled companies after completing this offering, but will rely on the exemption available for foreign private issuers to follow its home country governance practices instead. See “— Lotus Technology is a foreign private issuer within the meaning of the rules under the Exchange Act, and as such is exempt from certain provisions applicable to U.S. domestic public companies.” If Lotus Technology ceases to be a foreign private issuer or if it cannot rely on the home country governance practice exemption for any reason, Lotus Technology may decide to invoke the exemptions available for a controlled company as long as it remains a controlled company.
